tools.name: create_widget_blueprint
tools.description:
Create a new UMG Widget Blueprint asset (WBP_) with an optional root widget class.

Widget Blueprints have TWO systems (analogous to Blueprint actors):
  1. Widget Tree: Visual hierarchy of UWidget objects (CanvasPanel, TextBlock, Button, etc.)
     — managed via add_widget, set_widget_property, set_widget_slot
  2. Widget Blueprint Graph: Standard K2 Blueprint logic (OnClicked bindings, variable set/get)
     — populated via inject_blueprint_nodes_t3d with asset_path pointing to the WBP

Naming convention: WBP_ prefix (e.g. WBP_HealthBar, WBP_MainMenu, WBP_Settings)

Workflow:
1. create_widget_blueprint — create the asset with root panel
2. add_widget — build the widget hierarchy (TextBlock, Button, Image, ProgressBar, etc.)
3. set_widget_slot — configure layout: anchors, offsets, padding, alignment, fill rules
4. set_widget_property — set text, colors, visibility, percent
5. set_widget_font — configure fonts on text widgets
6. set_widget_brush — configure images/brushes on Image/Button/Border widgets
7. bind_widget_event — bind OnClicked, OnHovered, etc. to Blueprint functions
8. compile_widget_blueprint — verify no errors
9. inject_blueprint_nodes_t3d — wire click/hover event logic in the graph

asset_path.description: Content path for the Widget Blueprint, e.g. /Game/UI/WBP_MainMenu. MUST start with /Game/ and be non-empty.

root_widget_class.description:
Class for the root container widget. Default: CanvasPanel.
Options: CanvasPanel (freely positioned UI), VerticalBox (vertical auto-stack), HorizontalBox (horizontal auto-stack), SizeBox, Overlay (z-stacked layers), GridPanel, ScrollBox, WrapBox.
Use CanvasPanel for HUD overlays with absolute positioning.
Use VerticalBox/HorizontalBox for auto-layout menus and lists.

parent_class.description: Parent class for the Widget Blueprint. Default: UserWidget. Use 'CommonActivatableWidget' for full-screen menus with input routing (requires CommonUI plugin).

tools.name: add_widget
tools.description:
Add a UMG widget to the widget tree of an existing Widget Blueprint.

The parent_widget MUST be a panel widget (a container that accepts children):

Panel widgets (can be parents):
  CanvasPanel — absolute positioning with anchors
  VerticalBox — vertical auto-stack
  HorizontalBox — horizontal auto-stack
  GridPanel — grid layout
  UniformGridPanel — uniform grid layout
  ScrollBox — scrollable container
  Overlay — z-stacked layers
  WidgetSwitcher — tab-style visibility switcher
  WrapBox — wrapping horizontal flow

Content widgets (single child slot — can still be parent):
  SizeBox — enforces min/max size on ONE child
  ScaleBox — scales ONE child
  Border — decorative border around ONE child
  Button — clickable, contains ONE child (usually a TextBlock)
  BackgroundBlur — blurred background for ONE child
  InvalidationBox — caches rendering of ONE child
  RetainerBox — render-to-texture for ONE child

Leaf widgets (cannot be parents):
  TextBlock — display text
  RichTextBlock — styled text with markup
  Image — display a texture/material
  ProgressBar — fill-based value display
  Slider — draggable numeric value
  CheckBox — boolean toggle
  EditableTextBox — single-line text input
  MultiLineEditableTextBox — multi-line text input
  ComboBoxString — dropdown selection
  SpinBox — numeric spinner
  Spacer — empty spacing
  Throbber — animated loading dots
  CircularThrobber — rotating loading indicator
  ExpandableArea — collapsible section

Call get_widget_tree FIRST to see current widget names and which are panels.

IMPORTANT: After adding a widget to a CanvasPanel, you MUST call set_widget_slot to configure
anchors, position, and size — otherwise the widget will have zero size and be invisible.

asset_path.description: Content path of the Widget Blueprint to modify

widget_class.description: Widget class to add. See tool description for full list of panels, content widgets, and leaf widgets.

widget_name.description: Unique name for the widget in the tree, e.g. TitleText, HealthBar, PlayButton. Used to reference this widget in other tools.

parent_widget.description: Name of the parent widget to add this to. Must be a panel or content widget. Use get_widget_tree to find names. If omitted, attaches to root panel.

tools.name: set_widget_slot
tools.description:
Configure the layout slot of a widget within its parent panel.

Every widget inside a panel has a SLOT that controls its layout. The slot type depends on the parent:

## CanvasPanel Slots (UCanvasPanelSlot)
For widgets inside a CanvasPanel — control position, size, and anchoring:
  anchors_min — FVector2D: anchor minimum (0,0)=top-left (0.5,0.5)=center (1,1)=bottom-right
  anchors_max — FVector2D: anchor maximum. Same as min for fixed position; (1,1) for stretch-to-fill
  offsets — FMargin: position and size offsets from anchor (Left=X, Top=Y, Right=Width, Bottom=Height when not stretching; all=padding when stretching)
  alignment — FVector2D: pivot point (0.5,0.5)=center
  auto_size — bool: auto-size to content
  z_order — int: draw order (higher = on top)

Common anchor presets:
  Fixed top-left: anchors_min=(0,0), anchors_max=(0,0), offsets=(X,Y,Width,Height)
  Centered: anchors_min=(0.5,0.5), anchors_max=(0.5,0.5), alignment=(0.5,0.5)
  Stretch-to-fill: anchors_min=(0,0), anchors_max=(1,1), offsets=(padding all sides)
  Stretch horizontal: anchors_min=(0,0.5), anchors_max=(1,0.5)
  Top bar: anchors_min=(0,0), anchors_max=(1,0), offsets=(0,0,0,Height)

## VerticalBox / HorizontalBox Slots
  padding — FMargin: space around widget (Left,Top,Right,Bottom)
  size_rule — string: 'Auto' (shrink to content) or 'Fill' (expand to fill available space)
  h_align — string: Left, Center, Right, Fill
  v_align — string: Top, Center, Bottom, Fill

## Overlay Slots
  padding — FMargin
  h_align — string: Left, Center, Right, Fill
  v_align — string: Top, Center, Bottom, Fill

## GridPanel Slots
  row, column — int: grid cell position
  row_span, column_span — int: how many cells to span
  padding — FMargin
  h_align, v_align — string

IMPORTANT: This tool is MANDATORY when adding widgets to a CanvasPanel.
Without it, widgets have zero size and are invisible.

asset_path.description: Content path of the Widget Blueprint

widget_name.description: Exact name of the widget whose slot to configure

anchors_min.description: Anchor minimum as 'X,Y' (CanvasPanel only). E.g. '0,0' for top-left, '0.5,0.5' for center, '0,0' with anchors_max='1,1' for stretch-to-fill.

anchors_max.description: Anchor maximum as 'X,Y' (CanvasPanel only). Same as anchors_min for fixed position. '1,1' for stretch.

offsets.description: Offsets as 'Left,Top,Right,Bottom' (CanvasPanel only). For fixed position: Left=X, Top=Y, Right=Width, Bottom=Height. For stretch: all values are padding.

alignment.description: Alignment pivot as 'X,Y' (CanvasPanel only). '0.5,0.5' centers the widget on its anchor point.

auto_size.description: Auto-size to content (CanvasPanel only). Default: false.

z_order.description: Draw order (CanvasPanel only). Higher = drawn on top.

padding.description: Padding as 'Left,Top,Right,Bottom' or single value for uniform. (All slot types)

row_span.description: Number of rows to span (GridPanel only)

column_span.description: Number of columns to span (GridPanel only)

tools.name: set_widget_property
tools.description:
Set a property on a named widget inside a Widget Blueprint via C++ reflection.

Property names are exact C++ UPROPERTY names (case-sensitive). For complex types like fonts and brushes, prefer the dedicated set_widget_font and set_widget_brush tools.

Common properties by widget type:

TextBlock:
  Text — the displayed string (plain text, e.g. 'Hello World')
  Justification — Left, Center, Right
  AutoWrapText — true/false
  WrapTextAt — float (0 = no wrap)
  MinDesiredWidth — float
  LineHeightPercentage — float (1.0 = default)
  TextTransformPolicy — None, ToLower, ToUpper

Button:
  BackgroundColor — linear color: '(R=0.2,G=0.6,B=1.0,A=1.0)'
  IsFocusable — true/false

Image:
  ColorAndOpacity — tint color: '(R=1.0,G=1.0,B=1.0,A=1.0)'

ProgressBar:
  Percent — 0.0 to 1.0 fill value
  FillColorAndOpacity — bar color
  bIsMarquee — true/false for indeterminate progress

Slider:
  Value — 0.0 to 1.0
  MinValue, MaxValue — range
  StepSize — increment step
  SliderBarColor, SliderHandleColor — colors

CheckBox:
  IsChecked — true/false

EditableTextBox:
  Text — initial text
  HintText — placeholder text
  IsPassword — true/false
  IsReadOnly — true/false

Border:
  ContentColorAndOpacity — content tint
  BrushColor — border color
  Padding — '(Left=5,Top=5,Right=5,Bottom=5)'

SizeBox:
  WidthOverride, HeightOverride — float (0 = no override)
  MinDesiredWidth, MinDesiredHeight — float
  MaxDesiredWidth, MaxDesiredHeight — float

Spacer:
  Size — '(X=20,Y=10)'

Any widget:
  Visibility — Visible, Collapsed, Hidden, HitTestInvisible, SelfHitTestInvisible
  bIsEnabled — true/false
  ToolTipText — hover tooltip string
  RenderOpacity — 0.0 (transparent) to 1.0 (opaque)
  Clipping — Inherit, ClipToBounds, ClipToBoundsWithoutIntersecting, ClipToBoundsAlways, OnDemand

asset_path.description: Content path of the Widget Blueprint

widget_name.description: Exact name of the target widget (as shown in get_widget_tree)

property_name.description: Exact C++ property name. Examples: Text, ColorAndOpacity, Visibility, Percent, bIsEnabled, BackgroundColor, Justification

property_value.description: String-serialized value. Examples: 'Hello World' for text, '(R=1.0,G=0.5,B=0.0,A=1.0)' for color, 'Visible' for visibility, 'true'/'false' for booleans, '0.75' for floats

tools.name: set_widget_font
tools.description:
Set the font on a text widget (TextBlock, EditableTextBox, RichTextBlock, MultiLineEditableTextBox).

UE5 fonts use FSlateFontInfo which is complex to set via raw reflection. This tool provides a clean interface.

Available engine fonts (no import needed):
  Roboto — default UE font (Regular, Bold, Italic, Light, Medium, Black)
  DroidSansMono — monospace font

For custom fonts, import a .ttf/.otf into the project first, then reference the asset path.

asset_path.description: Content path of the Widget Blueprint

widget_name.description: Name of the text widget to configure

font_family.description: Font family name or asset path. Built-in: 'Roboto' (default). For custom fonts, use the asset path e.g. '/Game/Fonts/MyFont'

font_size.description: Font size in points. Common sizes: 12 (body), 16 (subtitle), 24 (title), 36 (heading), 48 (display). Default: 12

typeface.description: Typeface style: Regular, Bold, Italic, Light, Medium, Black. Default: Regular

color.description: Text color as '(R=1.0,G=1.0,B=1.0,A=1.0)'. Only for TextBlock (sets ColorAndOpacity).

shadow_offset.description: Drop shadow offset as '(X=1,Y=1)'. Set to '(X=0,Y=0)' for no shadow.

tools.name: set_widget_brush
tools.description:
Set a brush (image/texture/solid color) on an Image, Button, Border, or ProgressBar widget.

FSlateBrush is a complex struct that is difficult to set via raw reflection. This tool provides a clean interface.

Brush targets by widget:
  Image — 'Brush' (the displayed image)
  Button — 'Normal', 'Hovered', 'Pressed', 'Disabled' (appearance states)
  Border — 'Background' (border background)
  ProgressBar — 'FillImage' (the fill bar brush)

For solid colors (no texture), just set tint_color and leave texture_path empty.
For textures, set texture_path to a Texture2D asset in the project.

asset_path.description: Content path of the Widget Blueprint

widget_name.description: Name of the widget to set brush on

brush_target.description: Which brush to set. Image: 'Brush'. Button: 'Normal', 'Hovered', 'Pressed', 'Disabled'. Border: 'Background'. ProgressBar: 'FillImage'. Default: 'Brush'.

texture_path.description: Asset path of the texture, e.g. '/Game/Textures/T_ButtonNormal'. Leave empty for solid color.

tint_color.description: Tint/color as '(R=1.0,G=1.0,B=1.0,A=1.0)'. For solid color brushes (no texture), this IS the color.

image_size.description: Desired image size as '(X=64,Y=64)'. Affects the brush draw size.

draw_as.description: How to draw: 'Box' (9-slice), 'Border' (border only), 'Image' (stretch to fill), 'NoDrawType' (invisible). Default: Image.

margin.description: 9-slice margin as '(Left=0.25,Top=0.25,Right=0.25,Bottom=0.25)'. Only used with draw_as=Box.

tools.name: bind_widget_event
tools.description:
Bind a widget event (OnClicked, OnHovered, etc.) to a custom event in the Widget Blueprint's EventGraph.

This creates a K2Node_ComponentBoundEvent node in the EventGraph that fires when the widget event occurs. After binding, you can use inject_blueprint_nodes_t3d to wire logic from the event node.

Supported events by widget type:
  Button: OnClicked, OnPressed, OnReleased, OnHovered, OnUnhovered
  Slider: OnValueChanged, OnMouseCaptureBegin, OnMouseCaptureEnd
  CheckBox: OnCheckStateChanged
  EditableTextBox: OnTextChanged, OnTextCommitted
  ComboBoxString: OnSelectionChanged, OnOpening
  SpinBox: OnValueChanged, OnValueCommitted

The tool returns the created event node's name (e.g. 'K2Node_ComponentBoundEvent_0') which you can use as a starting point for inject_blueprint_nodes_t3d to wire subsequent logic.

asset_path.description: Content path of the Widget Blueprint

widget_name.description: Name of the widget to bind the event on (e.g. 'PlayButton')

event_name.description: Name of the delegate event to bind. Button: OnClicked, OnPressed, OnReleased, OnHovered, OnUnhovered. Slider: OnValueChanged. CheckBox: OnCheckStateChanged. EditableTextBox: OnTextChanged, OnTextCommitted.

tools.name: remove_widget
tools.description:
Remove a widget from the widget tree of a Widget Blueprint.

If the widget is a panel with children, all children are also removed.
If the widget is the root, the tree becomes empty (rootless).
Cannot be undone within this tool — use checkpoint restore to undo.

asset_path.description: Content path of the Widget Blueprint

widget_name.description: Name of the widget to remove

tools.name: get_widget_tree
tools.description:
Read-only query: return the full widget hierarchy of a Widget Blueprint as JSON.

Returns a list of all widgets with:
  - name: exact widget name (use this in other widget tools)
  - class: widget class name
  - is_root: whether this is the root widget
  - is_panel: true = can be used as parent_widget in add_widget
  - child_count: number of direct children (for panels)
  - slot_type: the slot class (e.g. CanvasPanelSlot, VerticalBoxSlot)
  - parent: name of the parent widget (null for root)

Call this BEFORE add_widget, set_widget_slot, or set_widget_property to know exact widget names and hierarchy.

asset_path.description: Content path of the Widget Blueprint to inspect

tools.name: compile_widget_blueprint
tools.description:
Compile a Widget Blueprint and return all errors and warnings.

Widget Blueprints use the same compiler as regular Blueprint actors. Call after building the widget tree, setting slot properties, and wiring graph logic to verify correctness.

Also reports the widget count and root widget type for verification.

asset_path.description: Content path of the Widget Blueprint to compile
